was the 16th season of [FIA](/wiki/FIA "FIA") [Formula One](/wiki/Formula_One "Formula One") motor racing. It featured the 13th [World Championship of Drivers](/wiki/List_of_Formula_One_World_Drivers%27_Champions "List of Formula One World Drivers' Champions"), the 5th [International Cup for F1 Manufacturers](/wiki/List_of_Formula_One_World_Constructors%27_Champions "List of Formula One World Constructors' Champions"), and numerous non-championship [Formula One](/wiki/Formula_One "Formula One") races. The World Championship was contested over nine races between 20 May and 29 December 1962.

[BRM](/wiki/BRM "BRM") won the [International Cup for F1 Manufacturers](/wiki/International_Cup_for_F1_Manufacturers "International Cup for F1 Manufacturers") with the [BRM P48/57](/wiki/BRM_P48/57 "BRM P48/57") & [P57](/wiki/BRM_P57 "BRM P57").

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/5/5b/Taylor_at_1962_Dutch_Grand_Prix.jpg/250px-Taylor_at_1962_Dutch_Grand_Prix.jpg)](/wiki/File:Taylor_at_1962_Dutch_Grand_Prix.jpg)

[Lotus](/wiki/Team_Lotus "Team Lotus") finished second in the International Cup for F1 Manufacturers with the [Lotus 24](/wiki/Lotus_24 "Lotus 24") and [25](/wiki/Lotus_25 "Lotus 25").

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/85/Bruce_McLaren_at_1962_Dutch_Grand_Prix.jpg/250px-Bruce_McLaren_at_1962_Dutch_Grand_Prix.jpg)](/wiki/File:Bruce_McLaren_at_1962_Dutch_Grand_Prix.jpg)

[Cooper](/wiki/Cooper_Car_Company "Cooper Car Company") finished third in the International Cup for F1 Manufacturers with the [Cooper T53](/wiki/Cooper_T53 "Cooper T53"), [T55](/wiki/Cooper_T55 "Cooper T55") & [T60](/wiki/Cooper_T60 "Cooper T60").

The **1962 Formula One season** was the 16th season of [FIA](/wiki/FIA "FIA") [Formula One](/wiki/Formula_One "Formula One") motor racing. It featured the 13th [World Championship of Drivers](/wiki/List_of_Formula_One_World_Drivers%27_Champions "List of Formula One World Drivers' Champions"), the 5th [International Cup for F1 Manufacturers](/wiki/List_of_Formula_One_World_Constructors%27_Champions "List of Formula One World Constructors' Champions"), and numerous non-championship [Formula One](/wiki/Formula_One "Formula One") races. The World Championship was contested over nine races between 20 May and 29 December 1962.

[Graham Hill](/wiki/Graham_Hill "Graham Hill") driving for [BRM](/wiki/British_Racing_Motors "British Racing Motors") won his first Drivers' Championship when rival [Jim Clark](/wiki/Jim_Clark "Jim Clark") retired from the last race.[[1]](#cite_note-1) BRM also won the Manufacturers' Championship for the first time, and it would be the only time.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

Double World Champion [Jack Brabham](/wiki/Jack_Brabham "Jack Brabham") formed his own team and debuted the [Brabham BT3](/wiki/Brabham_BT3 "Brabham BT3") in the [United States Grand Prix](/wiki/1962_United_States_Grand_Prix "1962 United States Grand Prix"), becoming the first ever F1 driver to score championship points in a car bearing his own name.

[Ricardo Rodríguez](/wiki/Ricardo_Rodr%C3%ADguez_(racing_driver) "Ricardo Rodríguez (racing driver)") suffered a fatal crash during [practice](/wiki/Formula_One_racing#Free_practice "Formula One racing") for his home race, the non-championship [Mexican Grand Prix](/wiki/1962_Mexican_Grand_Prix "1962 Mexican Grand Prix"). He had been the youngest ever [driver](/wiki/Racing_driver "Racing driver") for [Scuderia Ferrari](/wiki/Scuderia_Ferrari "Scuderia Ferrari"), but also became the youngest ever F1 driver to die.

### Team and driver changes

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=1962_Formula_One_season&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Team and driver changes")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/4/4e/Stirling_Moss_Goodwood_2011.jpg/250px-Stirling_Moss_Goodwood_2011.jpg)](/wiki/File:Stirling_Moss_Goodwood_2011.jpg)

[Stirling Moss](/wiki/Stirling_Moss "Stirling Moss") (pictured in 2011) had signed with [Ferrari](/wiki/Scuderia_Ferrari "Scuderia Ferrari") for 1962, but a pre-season accident meant the end of his racing career.

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/e/e9/Brabham_BT3_%281962%29_Solitude_Revival_2019_IMG_1616.jpg/250px-Brabham_BT3_%281962%29_Solitude_Revival_2019_IMG_1616.jpg)](/wiki/File:Brabham_BT3_(1962)_Solitude_Revival_2019_IMG_1616.jpg)

Double World Champion [Jack Brabham](/wiki/Jack_Brabham "Jack Brabham") set up his own team for 1962 and had his own chassis designed: the [BT3](/wiki/Brabham_BT3 "Brabham BT3") (pictured in 2019).

* Like in [1961](/wiki/1961_Formula_One_season "1961 Formula One season"), [Ferrari](/wiki/Scuderia_Ferrari "Scuderia Ferrari") entered most races with three cars. [Stirling Moss](/wiki/Stirling_Moss "Stirling Moss") was due to drive for them, but suffered an accident during the non-championship [Glover Trophy](/wiki/1962_Glover_Trophy "1962 Glover Trophy"). He went into a [coma](/wiki/Coma "Coma") for a month and the left side of his body was [paralysed](/wiki/Paralysis "Paralysis") for six months.[[3]](#cite_note-3)[[4]](#cite_note-4) [Giancarlo Baghetti](/wiki/Giancarlo_Baghetti "Giancarlo Baghetti") was granted the seat, having proven himself worthy by winning the [1961 French Grand Prix](/wiki/1961_French_Grand_Prix "1961 French Grand Prix") and multiple non-championship races in a privately run Ferrari.
* [Richie Ginther](/wiki/Richie_Ginther "Richie Ginther") moved from Ferrari to [BRM](/wiki/British_Racing_Motors "British Racing Motors"), where [Tony Brooks](/wiki/Tony_Brooks_(racing_driver) "Tony Brooks (racing driver)") had retired. The team introduced their own [V8](/wiki/V8 "V8") engine for this season, after they had had to rely on [Climax](/wiki/Coventry_Climax "Coventry Climax") engines the year before.
* [Lotus](/wiki/Team_Lotus "Team Lotus") hired [Taylor](/wiki/Trevor_Taylor_(racing_driver) "Trevor Taylor (racing driver)") after [Innes Ireland](/wiki/Innes_Ireland "Innes Ireland") was fired and went to the [BRP](/wiki/British_Racing_Partnership "British Racing Partnership") team (renamed *UDT Laystall Racing*).
* Double World Champion [Jack Brabham](/wiki/Jack_Brabham "Jack Brabham") started his own team, [Brabham](/wiki/Brabham "Brabham"), at first running a Lotus chassis but later debuting the [BT3](/wiki/Brabham_BT3 "Brabham BT3"), with which he became the first F1 driver ever to score points in a car bearing his own name. [Tony Maggs](/wiki/Tony_Maggs "Tony Maggs") replaced him at [Cooper](/wiki/Cooper_Car_Company "Cooper Car Company").
* [Bowmaker Racing](/wiki/Yeoman_Credit_Racing "Yeoman Credit Racing") (what would later become [Reg Parnell Racing](/wiki/Reg_Parnell_Racing "Reg Parnell Racing")) entered the season with a [Lola Mk4](/wiki/Lola_Mk4 "Lola Mk4"), the first ever F1 chassis by [Lola Cars](/wiki/Lola_Cars "Lola Cars").

#### Mid-season changes

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=1962_Formula_One_season&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Mid-season changes")]

* [Lorenzo Bandini](/wiki/Lorenzo_Bandini "Lorenzo Bandini") and [Willy Mairesse](/wiki/Willy_Mairesse "Willy Mairesse") joined [Scuderia Ferrari](/wiki/Scuderia_Ferrari "Scuderia Ferrari") on a part-time basis, expanding the squad to five cars for the [Italian Grand Prix](/wiki/1962_Italian_Grand_Prix "1962 Italian Grand Prix").
* After a brief appearance in [1956](/wiki/1956_Formula_One_season "1956 Formula One season"), [Emeryson](/wiki/Emeryson "Emeryson") was back on the grid as a works team. They ran a Lotus chassis once before debuting a self-designed car in the [British Grand Prix](/wiki/1962_British_Grand_Prix "1962 British Grand Prix").
* The [ENB](/wiki/Equipe_Nationale_Belge "Equipe Nationale Belge") team debuted a self-made chassis in the [German Grand Prix](/wiki/1962_German_Grand_Prix "1962 German Grand Prix"), powered by a [Maserati](/wiki/Maserati_in_motorsport "Maserati in motorsport") engine.

### Calendar changes

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=1962_Formula_One_season&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Calendar changes")]

* The [Monaco Grand Prix](/wiki/Monaco_Grand_Prix "Monaco Grand Prix") was moved back three weeks, making the [Dutch Grand Prix](/wiki/Dutch_Grand_Prix "Dutch Grand Prix") the season opener.
* The [French Grand Prix](/wiki/French_Grand_Prix "French Grand Prix") was moved from [Reims-Gueux](/wiki/Reims-Gueux "Reims-Gueux") to [Rouen-Les-Essarts](/wiki/Rouen-Les-Essarts "Rouen-Les-Essarts") for a year, as it was the tradition to do every five years (after [1952](/wiki/1952_Formula_One_season "1952 Formula One season") and [1957](/wiki/1957_Formula_One_season "1957 Formula One season")).
* The [South African Grand Prix](/wiki/South_African_Grand_Prix "South African Grand Prix") made its debut on the championship calendar. The race was held at the [Prince George Circuit](/wiki/Prince_George_Circuit "Prince George Circuit") as the season finale on 29 December.

## Championship report

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=1962_Formula_One_season&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Championship report")]

### Rounds 1 to 3

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=1962_Formula_One_season&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Rounds 1 to 3")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/d/d7/G._Hill_at_1962_Dutch_Grand_Prix.jpg/250px-G._Hill_at_1962_Dutch_Grand_Prix.jpg)](/wiki/File:G._Hill_at_1962_Dutch_Grand_Prix.jpg)

[Graham Hill](/wiki/Graham_Hill "Graham Hill") ([BRM](/wiki/British_Racing_Motors "British Racing Motors")) won the season opener, the [Dutch Grand Prix](/wiki/1962_Dutch_Grand_Prix "1962 Dutch Grand Prix").

The [Dutch Grand Prix](/wiki/1962_Dutch_Grand_Prix "1962 Dutch Grand Prix") hosted the season opener for the first time and was granted the honorary designation of *European Grand Prix* for this year. [Lotus](/wiki/Team_Lotus "Team Lotus") turned up with a revolutionary new chassis, the [Lotus 25](/wiki/Lotus_25 "Lotus 25") being the first car built around an [aluminium](/wiki/Aluminium "Aluminium") [monocoque](/wiki/Monocoque "Monocoque") instead of a [space frame](/wiki/Space_frame#Cars "Space frame"). However, it was [John Surtees](/wiki/John_Surtees "John Surtees") who took [pole position](/wiki/Pole_position "Pole position") in the privately run [Lola](/wiki/Lola_Cars "Lola Cars"), ahead of [Graham Hill](/wiki/Graham_Hill "Graham Hill") for [BRM](/wiki/British_Racing_Motors "British Racing Motors"). Lotus's [Jim Clark](/wiki/Jim_Clark "Jim Clark") qualified third, but this meant he had the inside line to the first corner and he managed to take the lead. [Dan Gurney](/wiki/Dan_Gurney "Dan Gurney") in the [Porsche](/wiki/Porsche_in_motorsport#Formula_One "Porsche in motorsport") had a wonderful start from eighth to take third behind Hill and ahead of Surtees. The top three were steady in the opening phase, but then, both Clark and Gurney ran into technical troubles. Around the same time, the front [wishbone](/w/index.php?title=Wishbone_(car)&action=edit&redlink=1 "Wishbone (car) (page does not exist)") broke on Surtees's Lola and he had a heavy crash but escaped unhurt. Graham Hill led away and scored the victory. [Bruce McLaren](/wiki/Bruce_McLaren "Bruce McLaren") ([Cooper](/wiki/Cooper_Car_Company "Cooper Car Company")) was running second before he retired with a broken [gearbox](/wiki/Gearbox "Gearbox"). Reigning champion [Phil Hill](/wiki/Phil_Hill "Phil Hill") ([Ferrari](/wiki/Scuderia_Ferrari "Scuderia Ferrari")) was running third at the time, inheriting second but later being overtaken by [Trevor Taylor](/wiki/Trevor_Taylor_(racing_driver) "Trevor Taylor (racing driver)") (Lotus).[[5]](#cite_note-5)

Jim Clark scored his first career [pole](/wiki/Pole_position "Pole position") in the [Monaco Grand Prix](/wiki/1962_Monaco_Grand_Prix "1962 Monaco Grand Prix"), ahead of Graham Hill and Bruce McLaren. But due to a confusing start procedure, it was [Willy Mairesse](/wiki/Willy_Mairesse "Willy Mairesse") in the Ferrari who went by, touching wheels with Clark and Hill in the process and locking up his wheels into the first corner. A chain reaction led to an accident with six drivers, three of whom retired on the spot. Mairesse took the lead but spun his car in the second [hairpin](/wiki/Hairpin "Hairpin"), making the order at the end of the first lap: McLaren, Graham Hill, Phil Hill. McLaren was relegated back to second by lap 7, and then third by Clark, who was recovering from a bad start, was setting multiple fastest laps and started challenging Hill for the lead. On lap 55, however, his [clutch](/wiki/Clutch "Clutch") gave out and he had to retire. But Hill was not yet out of the woods, with his BRM engine starting to smoke and McLaren, back in second, getting ever closer. On lap 93, Hill's engine gave up with a bang, although he still scored a point, as he was classified in sixth. McLaren was challenged by Phil Hill but held on to score his first win in two years. [Lorenzo Bandini](/wiki/Lorenzo_Bandini "Lorenzo Bandini") finished third in his first race for Ferrari.[[6]](#cite_note-6)

The [Belgian Grand Prix](/wiki/1962_Belgian_Grand_Prix "1962 Belgian Grand Prix") saw Graham Hill [qualify](/wiki/Formula_One_racing#Qualifying "Formula One racing") on pole, ahead of McLaren, Taylor and home hero Mairesse. In the first phase of the race, those four were fighting over the lead in close combat and their relative positions changed all the while. Clark had started in twelfth, but joined the leading group. On lap 11, Clark set the fastest lap and took the lead, with teammate Taylor holding back their rivals. Clark held on to take the win, but getting up to the final corner of lap 26, Taylor crashed with Mairesse, the Lotus cutting down a [telegraph pole](/wiki/Telegraph_pole "Telegraph pole") and the Ferrari landing upside down on fire. Both drivers were flung out and suffered minor injuries. Graham Hill finished second, Phil Hill third. [Ricardo Rodríguez](/wiki/Ricardo_Rodr%C3%ADguez_(racing_driver) "Ricardo Rodríguez (racing driver)") came home in fourth, becoming the youngest F1 driver ever to score points. This record stood until [Jenson Button](/wiki/Jenson_Button "Jenson Button") broke it in the [2000 Brazilian Grand Prix](/wiki/2000_Brazilian_Grand_Prix "2000 Brazilian Grand Prix").[[7]](#cite_note-7)

In the Drivers' Championship, [Graham Hill](/wiki/Graham_Hill "Graham Hill") ([BRM](/wiki/British_Racing_Motors "British Racing Motors")) was leading with 16 points, ahead of [1961](/wiki/1961_Formula_One_season "1961 Formula One season") champion [Phil Hill](/wiki/Phil_Hill "Phil Hill") ([Ferrari](/wiki/Scuderia_Ferrari "Scuderia Ferrari")) with 14 and [Jim Clark](/wiki/Jim_Clark "Jim Clark") ([Lotus](/wiki/Team_Lotus "Team Lotus")) with 9. In the Manufacturers' Championship, BRM went ahead with 16 points, ahead of Lotus (15) and Ferrari (14).

### Rounds 4 to 6

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=1962_Formula_One_season&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Rounds 4 to 6")]

The [French Grand Prix](/wiki/1962_French_Grand_Prix "1962 French Grand Prix") saw [Jim Clark](/wiki/Jim_Clark "Jim Clark") [qualify](/wiki/Formula_One_racing#Qualifying "Formula One racing") on [pole position](/wiki/Pole_position "Pole position") in his [Lotus](/wiki/Team_Lotus "Team Lotus"), ahead of championship leader [Graham Hill](/wiki/Graham_Hill "Graham Hill") for [BRM](/wiki/British_Racing_Motors "British Racing Motors") and [Bruce McLaren](/wiki/Bruce_McLaren "Bruce McLaren") for [Cooper](/wiki/Cooper_Car_Company "Cooper Car Company"). Although [Ferrari](/wiki/Scuderia_Ferrari "Scuderia Ferrari") had withdrawn from the event due to [strike actions](/wiki/Strike_action "Strike action"),[[8]](#cite_note-IMS1062-8) the first three rows were occupied by seven different teams and five different constructors. Fifth-starting [John Surtees](/wiki/John_Surtees "John Surtees") was in second after the first lap and challenged Hill for the lead, but had to pit when his engine had trouble picking up fuel. [Jackie Lewis](/wiki/Jackie_Lewis "Jackie Lewis") was lapped by Hill but then suddenly lost his [brakes](/wiki/Brake "Brake") and [rear-ended](/wiki/Rear-end_collision "Rear-end collision") the leader. Clark took the lead, but was caught by Hill within three laps, confirming to the Lotus team that their car was not running well, and they decided to retire from the race. Further drama ensued when Hill's BRM [engine](/wiki/Car_engine "Car engine") had jammed with ten laps to go, paving the way for [Dan Gurney](/wiki/Dan_Gurney "Dan Gurney") taking his first and [Porsche](/wiki/Porsche_in_motorsport#Formula_One "Porsche in motorsport")'s only win. [South African](/wiki/South_Africa "South Africa") [Tony Maggs](/wiki/Tony_Maggs "Tony Maggs") was second for Cooper, scoring his first podium, and [Richie Ginther](/wiki/Richie_Ginther "Richie Ginther") third for BRM.[[9]](#cite_note-9)

During the [British Grand Prix](/wiki/1962_British_Grand_Prix "1962 British Grand Prix"), Clark scored his third pole position of the year, this time ahead of Surtees and [Ireland](/wiki/Innes_Ireland "Innes Ireland"). The latter, however, could not get his Lotus to fire up and was passed at by everyone at the start. McLaren took over third place. The top three remained unchanged throughout the race, with Clark taking an unchallenged win and even getting close to lapping championship leader Hill, who came home in fourth.[[10]](#cite_note-10)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/82/1962-08-05_Graham_Hill%2C_Surtees%2C_Gurney_-_Hatzenbach.jpg/250px-1962-08-05_Graham_Hill%2C_Surtees%2C_Gurney_-_Hatzenbach.jpg)](/wiki/File:1962-08-05_Graham_Hill,_Surtees,_Gurney_-_Hatzenbach.jpg)

In the [German Grand Prix](/wiki/1962_German_Grand_Prix "1962 German Grand Prix"), the top three ([Hill](/wiki/Graham_Hill "Graham Hill"), [Surtees](/wiki/John_Surtees "John Surtees"), [Gurney](/wiki/Dan_Gurney "Dan Gurney")) finished within five seconds of each other.

The [German Grand Prix](/wiki/1962_German_Grand_Prix "1962 German Grand Prix") saw the debut of [Brabham](/wiki/Brabham "Brabham")'s first F1 chassis, designed by [Ron Tauranac](/wiki/Ron_Tauranac "Ron Tauranac"). [Scuderia Ferrari](/wiki/Scuderia_Ferrari "Scuderia Ferrari"), having recovered from [strikes](/wiki/Strike_action "Strike action") in [Italy](/wiki/Italy "Italy"),[[8]](#cite_note-IMS1062-8) brought an upgraded chassis and gave it to [Lorenzo Bandini](/wiki/Lorenzo_Bandini "Lorenzo Bandini") to try it out. During [practice](/wiki/Formula_One_racing#Free_practice "Formula One racing"), [Carel Godin de Beaufort](/wiki/Carel_Godin_de_Beaufort "Carel Godin de Beaufort") was running his [Porsche](/wiki/Porsche "Porsche") with a large [camera](/wiki/Camera "Camera") mounted to the rear by the [German](/wiki/Germany "Germany") [television](/wiki/Television "Television") crew. The device fell off, however, and championship leader Hill was the unlucky victim, unable to avoid it, breaking his oil lines and quickly spinning off the track. Tony Maggs then slid on Hill's oil and had a similar accident, but both drivers were unhurt. Gurney started on pole, ahead of Hill and Clark, but the race was delayed for over an hour, after a sudden [downpour](/wiki/Rain "Rain") made the track incredibly slippery. When the race got underway, Clark stalled his engine and fell back, but he passed 17 cars on the first lap. The leading pair went side-by-side as they started the third lap and it was Hill who came out on top. Surtees had inherited third place from Clark and this top three got away from the rest. Surtees got second place when Gurney's [battery](/wiki/Automotive_battery "Automotive battery") came loose and the [American](/wiki/United_States "United States") had to secure by hand. At half-distance, Clark had recovered to fourth place, mastering the wet conditions and gaining at least five seconds per lap on the leading trio. After almost losing the car twice at high speed, however, the [Scot](/wiki/Scot "Scot") settled down. Surtees tried to push Hill into making a mistake but the [Brit](/wiki/Great_Britain "Great Britain") held on, taking the win, with Surtees and Gurney finishing within the next five seconds.[[11]](#cite_note-11)

In the Drivers' Championship, [Graham Hill](/wiki/Graham_Hill "Graham Hill") ([BRM](/wiki/British_Racing_Motors "British Racing Motors")) was still holding onto the lead with 28 points, ahead of [Jim Clark](/wiki/Jim_Clark "Jim Clark") ([Lotus](/wiki/Team_Lotus "Team Lotus")) with 21 and [John Surtees](/wiki/John_Surtees "John Surtees") ([Lola](/wiki/Lola_Cars "Lola Cars")) with 19. In the Manufacturers' Championship, BRM were leading with 31, ahead of Lotus (27) and [Cooper](/wiki/Cooper_Car_Company "Cooper Car Company") (23).

### Rounds 7 to 9

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=1962_Formula_One_season&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Rounds 7 to 9")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/2/21/Giancarlo_Baghetti_a_Monza_-_1962.jpg/250px-Giancarlo_Baghetti_a_Monza_-_1962.jpg)](/wiki/File:Giancarlo_Baghetti_a_Monza_-_1962.jpg)

[Ferrari](/wiki/Scuderia_Ferrari "Scuderia Ferrari") entered five cars in their [home race](/wiki/1962_Italian_Grand_Prix "1962 Italian Grand Prix"), two of which, including [Giancarlo Baghetti](/wiki/Giancarlo_Baghetti "Giancarlo Baghetti") (pictured), finished in the points.

The [Italian Grand Prix](/wiki/1962_Italian_Grand_Prix "1962 Italian Grand Prix") was run on the *road circuit* of [Monza](/wiki/Autodromo_Nazionale_di_Monza "Autodromo Nazionale di Monza"), abandoning the fearsome [banked](/wiki/Banked_turn "Banked turn") [oval](/wiki/Oval_track_racing "Oval track racing"). [Jim Clark](/wiki/Jim_Clark "Jim Clark"), second in the championship, [qualified](/wiki/Formula_One_racing#Qualifying "Formula One racing") on [pole position](/wiki/Pole_position "Pole position"), ahead of championship leader [Graham Hill](/wiki/Graham_Hill "Graham Hill") and his teammate [Richie Ginther](/wiki/Richie_Ginther "Richie Ginther"). Hill took the lead at the start and led a group eight cars. [Ferrari](/wiki/Scuderia_Ferrari "Scuderia Ferrari") had brought five cars to their home race but they were all down in the second group. Clark pitted with [transmission](/wiki/Manual_transmission "Manual transmission") problems, while Hill managed to create some space between him and the rest. BRM scored a comfortable 1–2, while close fighting and a light rain shower provided exciting battles, from which [Bruce McLaren](/wiki/Bruce_McLaren "Bruce McLaren") came up to complete the podium.[[12]](#cite_note-12)

F1 moved overseas for the [United States Grand Prix](/wiki/1962_United_States_Grand_Prix "1962 United States Grand Prix") at [Watkins Glen](/wiki/Watkins_Glen_International "Watkins Glen International"). In repeat of this year's [French GP](/wiki/1962_French_Grand_Prix "1962 French Grand Prix") and last year's [US GP](/wiki/1961_United_States_Grand_Prix "1961 United States Grand Prix"), Ferrari withdrew from this race and the next because of ongoing [strikes](/wiki/Strike_action "Strike action") in [Italy](/wiki/Italy "Italy").[[8]](#cite_note-IMS1062-8) Clark qualified on pole, ahead of Ginther and Hill, and the Lotus was fastest at the start. Hill went into second, trying everything to stay with his rival. This paid off on lap 12, when Clark was held up by a backmarker and Hill swooped by. On lap 19, however, Clark snatched the lead back and held on to take the win, ten seconds ahead of Hill. The rest of the field, all at least a lap down, were led by third-placed McLaren.[[13]](#cite_note-13)

Coming to the season finale, the [South African Grand Prix](/wiki/1962_South_African_Grand_Prix "1962 South African Grand Prix"), Clark had a nine-point deficit to Hill. If Clark would manage to win the race, then Hill could finish second at best, which meant that his result would not count - only the five best results of the season counted towards the championship. This would result in a tie on points, and Clark would win the title on countback - he would have four wins against Hill's three.

Clark started off well, scoring his sixth pole position of the season, with Hill starting alongside him. He held the lead at the start and then pulled away by a second per lap. He had an immense lead of half a minute when, suddenly, blue [smoke](/wiki/Smoke "Smoke") started pouring from his engine. After stopping in the [pits](/wiki/Pit_lane "Pit lane"), it was found that a bolt was missing from the [crankcase](/wiki/Crankcase "Crankcase"), which had allowed [oil](/wiki/Motor_oil "Motor oil") to leak out. The smoke was caused by the oil leaking on the [exhaust](/wiki/Exhaust_system "Exhaust system"). The Lotus mechanics did not have a fix, which meant Clark had to retire and give up the championship. Hill could cruise to the finish but still won the race almost 50 seconds ahead of McLaren and home hero [Tony Maggs](/wiki/Tony_Maggs "Tony Maggs").[[14]](#cite_note-14)

The Drivers' Championship was settled with [Graham Hill](/wiki/Graham_Hill "Graham Hill") ([BRM](/wiki/British_Racing_Motors "British Racing Motors")) on 42 points, winning his first title, ahead of [Jim Clark](/wiki/Jim_Clark "Jim Clark") ([Lotus](/wiki/Team_Lotus "Team Lotus")) on 30 and [Bruce McLaren](/wiki/Bruce_McLaren "Bruce McLaren") ([Cooper](/wiki/Cooper_Car_Company "Cooper Car Company")) on 27. The Manufacturers' Championship was won by BRM with 42 points, ahead of Lotus (36) and Cooper (29).

### Scoring system

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=1962_Formula_One_season&action=edit&section=12 "Edit section: Scoring system")]

Further information: [List of Formula One World Championship points scoring systems](/wiki/List_of_Formula_One_World_Championship_points_scoring_systems "List of Formula One World Championship points scoring systems")

Points were awarded to the top six classified finishers. Only the best five results counted towards the championship.

The International Cup for F1 Manufacturers only counted the points of the highest-finishing driver for each race. Additionally, like the Drivers' Championship, only the best five results counted towards the cup.

Numbers without parentheses are championship points; numbers in parentheses are total points scored. Points were awarded in the following system:

| Position | 1st | 2nd | 3rd | 4th | 5th | 6th |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Race | 9 | 6 | 4 | 3 | 2 | 1 |
| Source:[[15]](#cite_note-8WPointsSystems-15) | | | | | | |
